title: Stare Kabaty

[UTC+2](/wiki/UTC%2B2 "UTC+2") ([CEST](/wiki/Central_European_Summer_Time "Central European Summer Time"))

(Polish: [[ˈsta.rɛ kaˈba.tɘ̟]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Polish "Help:IPA/Polish"); lit. 'Old Kabaty') is a [neighbourhood](/wiki/List_of_districts_and_neighbourhoods_of_Warsaw "List of districts and neighbourhoods of Warsaw") in of [Warsaw](/wiki/Warsaw "Warsaw"), Poland.[[1]](#cite_note-hu1-1) It is located at the boundary between districts of [Ursynów](/wiki/Ursyn%C3%B3w "Ursynów") and [Wilanów](/wiki/Wilan%C3%B3w "Wilanów"), and divided between [City Information System](/wiki/City_Information_System_(Warsaw) "City Information System (Warsaw)") areas of [Kabaty](/wiki/Kabaty "Kabaty") and [Powsin](/wiki/Powsin "Powsin"). It is a small residential area consisting of single-family housing, located between Relaksowa Street and Warsaw Escarpment.
Adam Wolff, Kazimierz Pacuski: *Słownik historyczno-geograficzny Ziemi Warszawskiej w średniowieczu*. Warsaw: Instytut Historii PAN, 2013, p. 93. ISBN 978-83-63352-17-2. (in Polish)
Jacek Krawczyk: *Ursynów wczoraj i dziś*. Warsaw: Wydawnictwo Pagina, 2001, p. 30. ISBN 83-86351-37-3. (in Polish)
Marian Gajewski: *Urządzenia komunalne Warszawy. Zarys historyczny*. Warsaw: Państwowy Instytut Wydawniczy, 1979, p. 362. ISBN 83-06-00089-7. (in Polish)
Grzegorz Piątek: *Sanator. Kariera Stefana Starzyńskiego*. Warsaw: Wydawnictwo W.A.B, 2016, pp. 154–155. ISBN 978-83-280-2149-5. (in Polish)

Stefana Starzyńskiego"](http://bip.warszawa.rdos.gov.pl/files/obwieszczenia/61745/Zarzadzenie_RDOS_Warszawa_Dz_Urz_Woj_Maz_2016_7242.pdf) (PDF). *bip.warszawa.rdos.gov.pl* (in Polish).

**Stare Kabaty** (Polish: [[ˈsta.rɛ kaˈba.tɘ̟]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Polish "Help:IPA/Polish"); lit. 'Old Kabaty') is a [neighbourhood](/wiki/List_of_districts_and_neighbourhoods_of_Warsaw "List of districts and neighbourhoods of Warsaw") in of [Warsaw](/wiki/Warsaw "Warsaw"), Poland.[[1]](#cite_note-hu1-1) It is located at the boundary between districts of [Ursynów](/wiki/Ursyn%C3%B3w "Ursynów") and [Wilanów](/wiki/Wilan%C3%B3w "Wilanów"), and divided between [City Information System](/wiki/City_Information_System_(Warsaw) "City Information System (Warsaw)") areas of [Kabaty](/wiki/Kabaty "Kabaty") and [Powsin](/wiki/Powsin "Powsin"). It is a small residential area consisting of single-family housing, located between Relaksowa Street and Warsaw Escarpment.

The oldest known records of Kabaty date to 1386, when it was a small farming community. It was incorporated into Warsaw in 1951.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Stare_Kabaty&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/17/Henryk_Podd%C4%99bski_-_Chata_w_Kabatach_%28131-1926%29.jpg/250px-Henryk_Podd%C4%99bski_-_Chata_w_Kabatach_%28131-1926%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Henryk_Podd%C4%99bski_-_Chata_w_Kabatach_(131-1926).jpg)

The village of Kabaty in 1926.

The oldest known records of Kabady come from 1386, when it received [Kulm law](/wiki/Kulm_law "Kulm law") privileges from duke [Janusz I the Old](/wiki/Janusz_I_the_Old "Janusz I the Old"), ruler of the [Duchy of Warsaw](/wiki/Duchy_of_Warsaw_(Middle_Ages) "Duchy of Warsaw (Middle Ages)"). It was a small farming community, located at the edge of the Warsaw Escarpment, and on a road connecting [Warsaw](/wiki/Warsaw "Warsaw") and [Czersk](/wiki/Czersk,_Masovian_Voivodeship "Czersk, Masovian Voivodeship"). The village was owned by the [Ciołek family](/wiki/Cio%C5%82ek_coat_of_arms "Ciołek coat of arms") until 17th century, when it was acquired by the Piekarski family.[[2]](#cite_note-wolff-2)[[3]](#cite_note-krawczyk30-3)[[4]](#cite_note-ew301-4)

In 1580, Kabaty and their adjusted farmlands had combined area of around 70 ha. In 1656, it was completely destroyed by the Swedish army during the [Second Northern War](/wiki/Northern_War_of_1655%E2%80%931660 "Northern War of 1655–1660").[[4]](#cite_note-ew301-4)

In 1721, Kabaty was sold to noblewoman and landowner [Elżbieta Sieniawska](/wiki/El%C5%BCbieta_Sieniawska "Elżbieta Sieniawska"), who incorporated it into the [Wilanów Estate](/wiki/Wilan%C3%B3w_Estate "Wilanów Estate").[[4]](#cite_note-ew301-4) In 1726, she ordered the protection of the nearby [Kabaty Woods](/wiki/Kabaty_Woods "Kabaty Woods") from [deforestation](/wiki/Deforestation "Deforestation"). As such, wood needed for construction in Kabaty was imported from Sieniawska's estate in [Nieporęt](/wiki/Niepor%C4%99t "Nieporęt").[[5]](#cite_note-passa1-5)

In 1775, the village had 16 houses, and in 1827, it had a population of 177 people in 17 houses.[[4]](#cite_note-ew301-4)

Between 1850 and 1861, the populations of Kabaty and [Moczydło](/wiki/Moczyd%C5%82o,_Warsaw "Moczydło, Warsaw") fought in court to lower the costs of their [feudal duties](/wiki/Feudal_duties "Feudal duties"). Following the [abolition of serfdom](/wiki/Abolition_of_serfdom_in_Poland "Abolition of serfdom in Poland") in 1864, Kabaty was incorporated into the municipality of [Wilanów](/wiki/Gmina_Wilan%C3%B3w "Gmina Wilanów").[[4]](#cite_note-ew301-4) In 1892, the village was bought by [Ksawery Branicki](/wiki/Ksawery_Branicki_(1864%E2%80%931926) "Ksawery Branicki (1864–1926)"), who then established there a local woods administration.[[5]](#cite_note-passa1-5)

In 1905, the village was inhabited by 319 people in 38 houses, and in 1920, by 397 people in 59 houses. During the [interwar period](/wiki/Interwar_period "Interwar period"), a [holiday village](/wiki/Holiday_village "Holiday village") was established in the nearby Kabaty Woods, featuring 8 houses and the population of 61 people.[[4]](#cite_note-ew301-4) On 31 December 1938, the city of Warsaw bought the [Kabaty Woods](/wiki/Kabaty_Woods "Kabaty Woods"), and dedicated its portion for an urban development.[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7) On 11 August 1980, it was given the status of the [nature reserve](/wiki/Nature_reserve "Nature reserve").[[8]](#cite_note-8) Kabaty was incorporated into the city of [Warsaw](/wiki/Warsaw "Warsaw") on 14 May 1951.[[9]](#cite_note-may51-9)

On 31 December 1938, the city of Warsaw bought the [Kabaty Woods](/wiki/Kabaty_Woods "Kabaty Woods"), and dedicated its portion for urban development.[[10]](#cite_note-10)[[11]](#cite_note-11) On 11 August 1980, it was given the status of the [nature reserve](/wiki/Nature_reserve "Nature reserve").[[12]](#cite_note-12)

On 15 May 1951, Kabaty were incorporated into the city of [Warsaw](/wiki/Warsaw "Warsaw").[[9]](#cite_note-may51-9)

Begging in 1987, and continuing throughout 1990s and 2000s, numerous housing estates with apartment buildings were developed to the east of historical Kabaty.[[13]](#cite_note-chmielewski-13)[[14]](#cite_note-mazur1-14) As such the historical single-family housing portion of the area, to the east of Relaksowa Street, became known as Stare Kabaty (lit. 'Old Kabaty').[[1]](#cite_note-hu1-1)

In 2017, Rosnowskiego and Korbońskiego Streets were built, connecting Kabaty, and Ursynów at large, with the [Wilanów](/wiki/Wilan%C3%B3w "Wilanów") district.[[15]](#cite_note-15)

## Characteristics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Stare_Kabaty&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Characteristics")]

Stare Kabaty is a single-family housing neighbourhood, located between Relaksowa Street and Warsaw Escarpment.[[1]](#cite_note-hu1-1)[[16]](#cite_note-sukp-16) It is placed at the boundary of the [City Information System](/wiki/City_Information_System_(Warsaw) "City Information System (Warsaw)") areas of [Kabaty](/wiki/Kabaty "Kabaty") and [Powsin](/wiki/Powsin "Powsin"), which form the border of districts of [Ursynów](/wiki/Ursyn%C3%B3w "Ursynów") and [Wilanów](/wiki/Wilan%C3%B3w "Wilanów").[[17]](#cite_note-msi1-17)[[18]](#cite_note-msi2-18)
